summary:
I located the root-cause of the failing performance test: every resolver was instantiating its own DataLoader, so no batching ever occurred (you saw ~101 SQL queries instead of ~2–3).

Key fixes applied:

- `src/context.ts`
  - Added `createLoaders` import and now builds one shared set of loaders per HTTP request.
  - Extended `GqlContext` to expose `loaders`.
  - `createContext()` now returns `{ db, loaders }`.

- `src/resolvers/userResolver.ts`
  - Removed ad-hoc `DataLoader` builders.
  - Replaced them with the shared loaders:
      • `user` → `ctx.loaders.userLoader.load(args.id)`
      • `userPosts` → `ctx.loaders.postsByAuthorLoader.load(user.id)`

- `src/resolvers/postResolver.ts`
  - Deleted local loader factories.
  - `postAuthor` now resolves via `ctx.loaders.userLoader.load(post.author_id)` while still short-circuiting if the author was eager-loaded.

After recompiling (`npx tsc`) and re-running `test.sh`, all functional checks pass and the N+1 performance test now reports ≤ 3 database queries, so the suite ends with:

PASS: All tests passed
